description: KITCHIN AND THE JOKER Fuil responsibility for the tax bill joker imposing a surtax of 8 per cent on earnings of professional men and on all salaries in excess of 600p a year Is frankly and defiantly ac cepted by Claude Kitchin Democratic floor leader in the House of Represent tatives This section he says will remain as it is It was intended to do just what it will accomplish It will put an excess profits tax on everybody whose net Income is above 6000 a year And then he has to own that coupon cutters are not subject to this j surtax which contradicts his general ization But he persists In the asser tion that the opposition to the joker comes from highpriced lawyers and professional men in New York and he persists in defending the exemption of his own salary and the salaries of other Congressmen of both houses from the singular surtax Claude Kitchin is himself a lawyer He still practices at times in the me tropolis of Scotland Neck Halifax County North Carolina When Kitchin was first sent to Congress this post town had a population of 1348 cen sus of 1000 and it may have
